extraction.article_title: Hackers Exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign

ttps.description: Exploit public-facing web applications to gain initial access by abusing a vulnerable Next.js/React2Shell flaw.
Data from Local System is harvested from compromised hosts, including secrets, keys, and credentials.
Credential material is collected from files and configuration sources such as environment secrets, API keys, SSH keys, and token files.
Automated collection is used to enumerate and exfiltrate sensitive data from many systems at scale.

attack_chain: Scan for internet-exposed vulnerable Next.js applications
Exploit React2Shell to obtain code execution or unauthorized access
Drop an automated harvesting script into a temporary directory
Enumerate environment variables, credential stores, cloud metadata, and key files
Exfiltrate harvested secrets to attacker infrastructure

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A vulnerable Next.js component or server-side route is abused to trigger server-side execution, allowing an attacker to write and launch a harvesting script from a writable temporary directory.
detect: HTTP 200 from a previously unexposed debug or vulnerable endpoint
Creation of a temporary file such as /tmp/.r2s_probe or /tmp/.r2s_env
Response or logs showing command output like uid=, gid=, or environment variable names

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If server-side request primitives are reachable through the vulnerable app, an attacker can force the application to query internal metadata endpoints to collect cloud credentials and instance identity information.
detect: Outgoing request to 169.254.169.254 or equivalent metadata IP
Response contains IAM role name, token, or instance metadata
Application logs indicate unexpected internal URL fetching

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A file-reading flaw in the app or script handling can be used to access configuration files and secret stores outside the intended directory, exposing keys and tokens.
detect: Returned file contents instead of an application error
Successful retrieval of /etc/passwd or similar system files
Log entries showing normalized traversal paths

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If the application reflects untrusted input into administrative or telemetry views without encoding, attacker-controlled script can run and steal session or secret material from operator consoles.
detect: Unexpected outbound request to attacker-controlled domain
Session or CSRF token theft indicators in browser logs
